question: [File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/config.go -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: loadNodeMapFromEnv()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a crafted HIERO_MIRROR_ROSETTA_NODES value pointing to attacker-controlled IP:port endpoints with valid-looking account IDs (e.g., '1.2.3.4:50211:0.0.3'), causing LoadConfig() to populate mirrorConfig.Rosetta.Nodes with attacker-controlled nodes, so that the Rosetta construction service submits transactions to and reads history from a fake Hashgraph node, resulting in fabricated transaction history being served to exchanges?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/config.go -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: loadNodeMapFromEnv()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a HIERO_MIRROR_ROSETTA_NODES value with a node account ID of '0.0.0' (zero account), which passes the hiero.AccountIDFromString() parse check but maps to an invalid/nonexistent consensus node, causing the SDK client to route all transaction submissions to a null node and silently drop or misroute history queries, corrupting the view of Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/config.go -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: loadNodeMapFromEnv()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_ROSETTA_NODES to a comma-separated list with duplicate IP:port keys (e.g., '1.2.3.4:50211:0.0.3,1.2.3.4:50211:0.0.4'), causing the nodeInfoMap to silently overwrite the first entry with the second, so that the resulting NodeMap contains an attacker-chosen account ID for that endpoint, redirecting Hashgraph history queries to a node with a different identity than intended?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/config.go -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: LoadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_ROSETTA_API_CONFIG to a path pointing to an attacker-crafted YAML file (e.g., a world-readable temp file) that overrides hiero.mirror.rosetta.network to 'mainnet' while the underlying database contains testnet history, causing the Rosetta API to serve testnet Hashgraph history labeled as mainnet to exchanges, enabling history confusion attacks?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/config.go -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: mergeExternalConfigFile()] Can an unprivileged external user place a malicious application.yml in the current working directory of the Rosetta process (e.g., via a shared volume or writable mount), which mergeExternalConfigFile() silently merges via v.AddConfigPath('.'), overriding the nodes map to point to attacker-controlled consensus nodes and causing all Hashgraph history served by the Rosetta API to originate from a fake ledger?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/config.go -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: LoadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set the environment variable HIERO_MIRROR_ROSETTA_NETWORK to 'mainnet' (overriding the embedded default via v.AutomaticEnv()), while the mirror node database is synced to testnet, causing the Rosetta API to advertise and serve testnet Hashgraph history under the mainnet network identifier, leading exchanges to accept fabricated or mismatched ledger history?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/config.go -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: LoadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_ROSETTA_ONLINE to 'false' via environment variable (picked up by v.AutomaticEnv()), switching the Rosetta server to offline mode, which causes it to stop querying the live mirror node database and serve stale or empty Hashgraph history to all API consumers, effectively freezing the visible ledger state?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/config.go -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: LoadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_ROSETTA_DB_HOST to an attacker-controlled PostgreSQL server via environment variable, causing the Rosetta API to connect to a fake mirror node database containing fabricated Hashgraph history (forged blocks, transactions, balances), which is then served as authoritative ledger data to exchanges?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/config.go -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: LoadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_ROSETTA_DB_PASSWORD to an incorrect value via environment variable, causing the Rosetta API to fail database authentication and fall back to serving empty or error responses for all Hashgraph history queries, resulting in a complete denial of ledger history availability?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/config.go -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: nodeMapDecodeHookFunc()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a YAML config (via HIERO_MIRROR_ROSETTA_API_CONFIG) with a nodes map where the account ID value is a valid-format string like '0.0.3' but the IP:port key is an attacker-controlled endpoint, causing nodeMapDecodeHookFunc() to decode it without validating that the endpoint actually belongs to the claimed account, allowing the attacker to impersonate a legitimate consensus node and serve forged Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/config.go -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: loadNodeMapFromEnv()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_ROSETTA_NODES to a value with an extremely large number of comma-separated entries (e.g., 100,000 nodes), causing loadNodeMapFromEnv() to allocate a massive nodeInfoMap and exhaust process memory during LoadConfig(), resulting in a denial of service that prevents the Rosetta API from starting and serving any Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/config.go -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: LoadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_ROSETTA_NODEREFRESHINTERVAL to '0s' or a negative duration via environment variable, causing the SDK client's network update period to be set to zero or an invalid value, which may trigger continuous or immediate address book refresh loops that flood the network with address book queries and corrupt the node list used for Hashgraph history retrieval?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/config.go -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: LoadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_ROSETTA_NODEREFRESHINTERVAL to an extremely large value (e.g., '876000h'), causing the SDK client to never refresh the network address book, so that if the legitimate node set changes (e.g., nodes are rotated), the Rosetta API continues routing to stale or decommissioned nodes, serving outdated or incorrect Hashgraph history indefinitely?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/config.go -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: LoadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_ROSETTA_COMMON_SHARD and HIERO_MIRROR_ROSETTA_COMMON_REALM (or the equivalent env keys) to arbitrary integers via environment variable, causing the Rosetta API to construct SubNetworkIdentifier values that do not match the actual shard/realm of the underlying Hashgraph history, leading exchanges to accept history from the wrong shard as if it were the correct one?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/config.go -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: mergeExternalConfigFile()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that mergeExternalConfigFile() silently ignores ConfigFileNotFoundError but propagates all other errors, by placing a syntactically valid but semantically malicious YAML file at './application.yml' that sets hiero.mirror.rosetta.nodes to attacker-controlled endpoints, causing the merged config to redirect all Hashgraph node communication to attacker infrastructure without any warning?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/config.go -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: LoadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_ROSETTA_API_CONFIG to '/proc/self/environ' or another special Linux pseudo-file, causing viper to attempt to parse process environment data as YAML, potentially leaking sensitive config values into logs via log.Infof() or causing a parse error that prevents the Rosetta API from loading, denying all access to Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/config.go -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: LoadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_ROSETTA_API_CONFIG to a path like '/dev/stdin' or a named pipe, causing mergeExternalConfigFile() to block indefinitely waiting for input, resulting in a denial of service that prevents the Rosetta API from initializing and serving any Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/config.go -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: LoadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_ROSETTA_RESPONSE_MAXTRANSACTIONSINBLOCK to '0' or '1' via environment variable, causing the Rosetta API to truncate block responses to zero or one transaction, making it impossible for exchanges to reconstruct complete Hashgraph history from block data and causing integrity failures in balance reconciliation?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/config.go -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: LoadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_ROSETTA_RESPONSE_MAXTRANSACTIONSINBLOCK to an extremely large integer (e.g., 2147483647) via environment variable, causing the Rosetta API to attempt to return unbounded numbers of transactions per block, exhausting memory and causing OOM crashes that deny access to Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/config.go -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: loadNodeMapFromEnv()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_ROSETTA_NODES to a value where the IP portion contains special characters or YAML injection sequences (e.g., '127.0.0.1:50211:0.0.3\\nhiero:\\n  mirror:\\n    rosetta:\\n      network: mainnet'), and if the value is later re-serialized or logged, cause secondary injection that alters the effective network configuration used to serve Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/config.go -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: LoadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_ROSETTA_DB_PORT to '0' or an invalid port number via environment variable, causing the Db.GetDsn() to produce a malformed DSN that connects to an unintended service or fails silently, resulting in the Rosetta API serving no Hashgraph history or connecting to a wrong data source?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/config.go -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: LoadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_ROSETTA_DB_NAME to an attacker-controlled database name via environment variable, causing the Rosetta API to connect to a different (possibly attacker-populated) database on the same PostgreSQL host, serving fabricated Hashgraph history from that database to all API consumers?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/config.go -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: LoadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_ROSETTA_DB_USERNAME to a different database user via environment variable, causing the Rosetta API to authenticate as a user with different (potentially broader or narrower) database permissions, enabling reads from tables containing unverified or manipulated Hashgraph history data?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/config.go -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: LoadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_ROSETTA_DB_STATEMENTTIMEOUT to '0' via environment variable, disabling query timeouts entirely, allowing slow or malicious database queries to hold connections indefinitely and starve legitimate Hashgraph history queries of database connections, causing a denial of service?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/config.go -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: LoadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_ROSETTA_DB_POOL_MAXOPENCONNECTIONS to '1' via environment variable, reducing the connection pool to a single connection, causing all concurrent Hashgraph history requests to serialize and queue, enabling a single slow request to block all other history queries and effectively deny service?
